rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Plan-mode activation, push-back mechanism, and auto-load filename surfaces all match compendium-verified facts (re-verified 2026-05-14).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts: Plan mode = Shift+Tab cycle OR /plan.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Frameworks-block names 'Shift+Tab cycle (CLI) or the mode dropdown (Desktop)'; leap-test #1 also names 'Shift+Tab cycle'. Matches compendium §6 verbatim — no Shift+P misclaim.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Auto-load is filename-based, not folder-based.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Plug Points + Artefact Contracts state auto-load semantics correctly: ./CLAUDE.local.md + ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md auto-load; ~/.claude/memory/<file>.md only loads via @import or named-path. Matches §6d.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Bridge text ('M3 opens fresh and runs security skills...') uses session-as-unit phrasing; no 'open Claude Code in' substitution drift.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Practitioner writing on employer's venue: label [practitioner direct, vendor venue].
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Klaassen on every.to labeled [practitioner direct, vendor venue] per the 2026-05-14 carve-out. Correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-type labels in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Plan-mode docs labeled [practitioner direct] in Frameworks block; Klaassen labeled [practitioner direct, vendor venue]; Martin treated as cultural-vocab (vision-layer, no URL required per §1 cultural-vocab carve-out).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cite within 6 months OR explicitly framed historical.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Willison piece (June 2025) explicitly framed as 'foundational framing... used as reference not as fresh evidence' — meets historical-context carve-out. OWASP framed as broader-checklist reference.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Zombie stat guard.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No round-number AI failure stats in file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Both referenced prompts (push-back-on-the-plan-4, ae101-m2-integrate-branch) carry no bracketed placeholders.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: One-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body lead-ins present for both prompts — 'Before saving, ask Claude whether anything from this session has made it to auto-load yet' and the integrate-branch lead-in.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination chip.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Expanded prompts render '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*' and '**Prompt** *(Claude Code, only if something earned itself)*' destination chips; matches §3 + §28.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No skill invocations.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Preamble before action — structural trigger.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Neither prompt invokes plan mode or carries 4+ named sub-steps — preamble-suppressor not required.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't restate session CWD inside the prompt body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompts use ./CLAUDE.local.md as relative subpath — no absolute paths, no cd commands.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in registry; module references via marker.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses {{prompt:push-back-on-the-plan-4}} and {{prompt:ae101-m2-integrate-branch}} markers; both registry entries exist with correct frontmatter.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No module-name refs in prompt prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Neither registry prompt body contains bare M-tokens. Module file body uses 'Module 1' / 'Module 2' / 'Module 3' references — exempt because rule 32 fires on prompt body, not host file body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Wide prompts split by logical move.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: ae101-m2-integrate-branch (3 logical moves: file-write, chat-output, null-case) is split into 3 paragraphs; push-back-on-the-plan-4 is single short prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism — every named tool/path exists.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Referenced exercises (push-back-on-the-plan, extract-the-task-shaping-rule) and lectures (when-a-plan-is-good, where-the-rule-could-live) all present in curriculum/exercises and curriculum/lectures. Reference docs (multi-session-git.md, claude-code-for-engineers.md) present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rule in memory that does not force is worse than no rule.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Save-the-rule beat uses ./CLAUDE.local.md (auto-load filename per platform §6d) — the rule fires next session because the location auto-loads.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs smoke-test.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No verification machinery built in this module — plan-mode push-back is the move; no judges/hooks/loops.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers as staged progression.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: push-back-on-the-plan-4 ('are these rules auto-loaded to each session context?') is the auto-load tier-1 surface-reveal move per pedagogy §37.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Push-back via 'keep planning with feedback' at the approval prompt
Ctrl+G plan-file edit flow named as a practitioner primitive (Watch-fors)

citations_checked.claim: Boris Cherny, Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes (YouTube)
Simon Willison, The lethal trifecta for AI agents (June 2025)
Plan mode reference — Anthropic Claude Code docs
What would have to be true — Roger Martin (HBR, Playing to Win)
